questions.q: What is the outermost part of the Earth's interior called?
questions.explanation: The crust is the outermost solid layer of the Earth.

questions.q: What is the main composition of the Earth's crust?
questions.explanation: The Earth's crust is primarily composed of ore minerals and rocks.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a layer of the Earth's interior?
questions.explanation: The atmosphere is not a layer of the Earth's interior.

questions.q: What is the primary function of the Earth's mantle?
questions.explanation: The Earth's mantle is responsible for generating heat through radioactive decay.

questions.q: What is the difference between the Earth's outer core and inner core?
opts: Outer core is liquid, inner core is solid
Outer core is solid, inner core is liquid
Outer core is liquid, inner core is liquid
Outer core is solid, inner core is solid
questions.explanation: The Earth's outer core is liquid, while the inner core is solid.

questions.q: Which of the following is a result of the Earth's internal heat?
questions.explanation: Volcanic eruptions are a result of the Earth's internal heat.

questions.q: What is the process called when the Earth's crust is broken and deformed?
questions.explanation: Folding and faulting are processes that occur when the Earth's crust is broken and deformed.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of the Earth's core?
opts: It is solid and has a high density
It is liquid and has a low density
It is solid and has a low density
It is liquid and has a high density
questions.explanation: The Earth's core is solid and has a high density.

questions.q: What is the role of the Earth's mantle in the Earth's plate tectonics?
opts: It is responsible for generating heat
It is responsible for storing water
It is responsible for producing earthquakes
It is responsible for moving tectonic plates
questions.explanation: The Earth's mantle plays a crucial role in moving tectonic plates through convection currents.

questions.q: How does the Earth's core affect the Earth's magnetic field?
opts: It has no effect on the magnetic field
questions.explanation: The Earth's core generates the Earth's magnetic field through the movement of molten iron.

questions.q: What is the process called when the Earth's mantle melts and rises to the surface?
questions.explanation: Volcanism is the process of the Earth's mantle melting and rising to the surface.

questions.q: Which of the following is a result of the Earth's core-mantle interaction?
questions.explanation: Tectonic plate movement is a result of the Earth's core-mantle interaction through convection currents.

questions.q: What is the role of the Earth's crust in the Earth's plate tectonics?
opts: It is responsible for generating heat
It is responsible for storing water
It is responsible for producing earthquakes
It is responsible for moving tectonic plates
questions.explanation: The Earth's crust plays a crucial role in producing earthquakes through faulting.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of the Earth's outer core?
opts: It is solid and has a high density
It is liquid and has a low density
It is solid and has a low density
It is liquid and has a high density
questions.explanation: The Earth's outer core is liquid and has a low density.

questions.q: What is the difference between the Earth's lithosphere and asthenosphere?
opts: Lithosphere is solid, asthenosphere is liquid
Lithosphere is liquid, asthenosphere is solid
Lithosphere is solid, asthenosphere is solid
Lithosphere is liquid, asthenosphere is liquid
questions.explanation: The lithosphere is the solid outer layer of the Earth, while the asthenosphere is the upper part of the mantle that is partially molten.

questions.q: Which of the following is a result of the Earth's thermal expansion?
questions.explanation: Tectonic plate movement is a result of the Earth's thermal expansion through convection currents.

questions.q: What is the role of the Earth's mantle in the Earth's thermal budget?
opts: It is responsible for generating heat
It is responsible for storing water
It is responsible for producing earthquakes
It is responsible for distributing heat
questions.explanation: The Earth's mantle plays a crucial role in distributing heat through convection currents.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of the Earth's magnetic field?
questions.explanation: The Earth's magnetic field is variable and changing due to the movement of molten iron in the Earth's core.

questions.q: What is the process called when the Earth's crust is broken and deformed through the movement of tectonic plates?
questions.explanation: Plate tectonics is the process of the Earth's crust being broken and deformed through the movement of tectonic plates.

questions.q: Which of the following is a result of the Earth's core-mantle interaction in the Earth's plate tectonics?
questions.explanation: Tectonic plate movement is a result of the Earth's core-mantle interaction through convection currents.
